verificationNote: Clauses paraphrased from BNM RMiT (November 2025). Verbatim text available in the dawuds/RMIT repository. Obtain authoritative text from BNM.

clauses.title: Cloud Services Risk Assessment
clauses.summary: Financial institutions must conduct a comprehensive risk assessment before adopting cloud services, addressing the inherent multi-tenant, shared-resource architecture of cloud computing.

riskAreas.description: Assess complexity of chosen deployment model (public, private, hybrid, multi-cloud)
Risks associated with migrating existing systems to cloud infrastructure
Location of cloud infrastructure including geo-political and legal risks that may impede regulatory compliance
Risks from shared infrastructure and potential data co-mingling with other tenants
Application portability, interoperability, and ability to migrate between providers
Ability to customise security configurations to ensure high-level data and system protection
Exposure to cyber-attacks via cloud service provider infrastructure
Ability to secure data following termination of the CSP relationship
Clear demarcation of responsibilities, limitations, and liability between institution and CSP
Ability to meet regulatory requirements and international standards on an ongoing basis

cspGuidance.aws: Use AWS Artifact for compliance reports; AWS Config + Security Hub for posture assessment; deploy in ap-southeast-1 (Singapore) or request Malaysia Local Zone
cspGuidance.azure: Use Azure Compliance Manager; Defender for Cloud regulatory compliance dashboard; deploy in Southeast Asia region
cspGuidance.gcp: Use Assured Workloads for compliance controls; SCC for posture; deploy in asia-southeast1 (Singapore)

clauses.title: Public Cloud Key Risks and Controls (Appendix 10)
clauses.markerMeaning: Should (guidance, but deviations must be justified)
clauses.summary: For critical systems on public cloud, financial institutions must consider common key risks and control measures specified in Appendix 10. Departures from these measures must be demonstrated to be at least as effective.

keyControls: Cloud governance framework with board oversight
Cloud security policy approved by senior management
Roles and responsibilities for cloud security clearly defined
Data classification applied to cloud-hosted data
Encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+)
Customer-managed encryption keys for critical data
MFA for all administrative and privileged access
Least privilege principle for cloud IAM
Privileged access management with just-in-time access
No public internet access for backend/database services
Private endpoints for CSP service access
Cloud audit logging enabled for all regions and services
Regular DR testing including cloud failover scenarios
RTO/RPO targets defined and tested for cloud workloads
CSP SOC 2 Type II report review
Cloud included in annual technology audit scope

cspGuidance.aws: AWS Security Hub CIS Benchmark; AWS Audit Manager for compliance frameworks; request SOC 2 via AWS Artifact
cspGuidance.azure: Microsoft Defender for Cloud regulatory compliance; Azure compliance offerings; request SOC 2 via Service Trust Portal
cspGuidance.gcp: SCC compliance module; Assured Workloads; request SOC 2 via GCP Compliance Reports Manager

clauses.title: Cloud Data Residency
clauses.summary: Critical customer data and its backups must reside in Malaysia. Where a Malaysian CSP region is not available, nearest approved region (Singapore) may be used subject to BNM approval. Metadata and derived analytics are also subject to data residency considerations.
requirements: Critical customer data must reside in Malaysia
Backup copies of critical customer data must also reside in Malaysia
Metadata and derived analytics must be considered for data residency
If no Malaysian CSP region exists, nearest approved region may be used with BNM notification
Region restriction policies must prevent data creation in non-approved regions
Monitor CSP service updates that may change data replication behaviour

cspRegionOptions.malaysiaRegion: No dedicated Malaysia region (Local Zone pending)
cspRegionOptions.notes: Use SCPs to restrict region usage
Use Azure Policy to restrict allowed locations
Use Organisation Policy resource location constraint
Deploy in KL region for data residency compliance
Deploy in KL region for data residency compliance

cspGuidance.aws: SCP: deny all regions except ap-southeast-1; enable AWS Config rule for resource region compliance; verify S3 replication settings
cspGuidance.azure: Azure Policy: allowedLocations = ['southeastasia']; verify no geo-redundant replication to non-approved regions
cspGuidance.gcp: Organisation Policy: constraints/gcp.resourceLocations = ['asia-southeast1']; verify Cloud Storage location settings

clauses.section: S17: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
clauses.title: First-Time Cloud Consultation with BNM
clauses.summary: Financial institutions must consult BNM before first-time adoption of public cloud for critical systems. Must demonstrate that risks have been adequately considered and addressed.

prerequisites.requirement: Comprehensive risk assessment per 10.50 and Appendix 10, using Appendix 7 Part A format
Written confirmation of readiness from CISO, senior management officer, or board-level committee chair (Appendix 7 Part B)
Third-party pre-implementation review covering Appendix 10 areas and Appendix 7 Part C for higher-risk services

clauses.higherRiskServices: Services involving processing or storage of customer information, or cross-border data transmission, require enhanced pre-implementation review (Appendix 7 Part C)

clauses.section: S17: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
clauses.title: Subsequent Cloud Adoption Notification
clauses.summary: For subsequent cloud adoptions for critical systems, financial institutions must notify BNM (not consult) provided they meet pre-conditions: no concerns from first consultation, enhanced risk framework, independent assurance, and adequate incident response.
preconditions: BNM raised no concerns during first-time consultation under 17.1
Technology risk management framework enhanced to manage cloud risks
Independent assurance established on cloud risk management framework
Incident response plans confirmed sufficient for adverse events

clauses.section: S17: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
clauses.title: Enhancement Notification Exemption
clauses.summary: Notification to BNM is not required for enhancements to existing cloud adoption that do not materially alter prior assessments and representations made to BNM.
clauses.keyConsideration: Institutions must assess whether an enhancement materially alters the risk profile. Adding a new CSP, moving to a new deployment model, or adding critical data to an existing cloud deployment would likely be considered material changes requiring notification.

clauses.section: S17: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
clauses.title: BNM Discretionary Direction
clauses.summary: BNM may direct any financial institution to consult or notify, or to observe Appendix 10/Appendix 9 guidance, including for non-critical systems or where pre-requisites have not been met. Institutions must comply promptly.
clauses.implication: BNM retains discretion to require the full consultation process even for non-critical systems. This means financial institutions should maintain a cloud governance posture that could withstand BNM scrutiny for any cloud deployment, not just critical systems.

clauses.section: S17: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
clauses.title: Cloud Adoption Roadmap in Annual Outsourcing Plan
clauses.summary: The cloud adoption roadmap (both critical and non-critical systems) must be included in the annual outsourcing plan submitted to BNM. Risk assessments must be documented and available for BNM review upon request.
requirements: Include cloud roadmap in annual outsourcing plan to BNM
Cover both critical and non-critical system cloud adoptions
Document risk assessments per 10.50 for cloud services
Make all documentation available for BNM review on request
evidence: Annual outsourcing plan with cloud section
